blocks.body: Future Continuous — will be + verb-ing for actions that will be in progress at a future moment, as background in the future, or in polite questions about plans.

{"level":"B1","intro":"In this section, you will learn Future Continuous and the pattern will be + verb-ing for actions that will be in progress at a certain point in the future.","rules":[{"label":"Form","color":"emerald","text":"Start with the formula subject + will be + verb-ing.","example":"This time tomorrow, I will be flying to Warsaw."},{"label":"Future moment","color":"blue","text":"This tense shows that an action will be in progress at a specific time in the future.","example":"At 8 p.m. tomorrow, we will be having dinner."},{"label":"Polite questions","color":"amber","text":"Future Continuous often sounds softer in polite questions about plans and arrangements.","example":"Will you be using the car later tonight?"}]}

{"title":"1. Lessons in order","intro":"Start with the form and use, then move to negatives, questions, and time expressions. Each card includes short rules from the matching lesson.","items":[{"label":"B1","title":"Forms and Use","subtitle":"will be + verb-ing and the main uses.","rules":[{"label":"Form","formula":"will be + V-ing","text":"The form is the same for every subject: will be + V-ing.","example":"I will be working. / They will be travelling."},{"label":"Use","formula":"future in progress","text":"Use it for an action in progress at a specific future moment.","example":"At 8 p.m. tomorrow, I will be studying."}]},{"label":"B1","title":"Negatives","subtitle":"won't be + verb-ing and what will not be happening.","rules":[{"label":"Negative","formula":"won't be + V-ing","text":"Use will not / won't be + V-ing for negatives.","example":"She won't be working tomorrow."},{"label":"Keep be","formula":"not be + V-ing","text":"After won't, keep be before V-ing.","example":"We won't be waiting outside."}]},{"label":"B1","title":"Questions and Short Answers","subtitle":"Will you be working...? / Yes, I will. / No, I won't.","rules":[{"label":"Question","formula":"Will + subject + be + V-ing?","text":"Put Will at the beginning and keep be before V-ing.","example":"Will you be working at 6?"},{"label":"Short answers","formula":"will / won't","text":"Short answers use will / won't.","example":"Yes, I will. / No, she won't."}]},{"label":"B1","title":"Time Expressions","subtitle":"this time tomorrow, at 8 p.m. tomorrow, when, while.","rules":[{"label":"Right now","formula":"this time tomorrow","text":"These markers show a process at a specific future moment.","example":"This time tomorrow, we will be flying."},{"label":"Temporary period","formula":"when / while","text":"Future Continuous often gives background for another future event.","example":"I will be cooking when you arrive."}]}]}

{"title":"2. Where you need this tense","sections":[{"label":"Action in progress at a future moment","color":"emerald","description":"Show that an action will still be happening at a particular time in the future.","examples":[{"en":"At noon tomorrow, I will be sitting on the train.","ua":"The action will still be in progress then."},{"en":"This time next week, they will be relaxing by the sea.","ua":"The process will already be happening at that future point."}]},{"label":"Future background","color":"blue","description":"Use Future Continuous as background for another event or future situation.","examples":[{"en":"When you arrive, we will be eating dinner.","ua":"One action is the background for another event."},{"en":"All day tomorrow, she will be working on the report.","ua":"This highlights a longer future process."}]},{"label":"Polite questions","color":"amber","description":"The tense often sounds softer when you ask about someone's plans or arrangements.","examples":[{"en":"Will you be staying at the hotel tonight?","ua":"A question about an arranged situation."},{"en":"Will they be joining us later?","ua":"A polite way to check the plan."}]}]}

{"title":"3. In short","items":["Future Continuous = will be + verb-ing.","Use this tense for actions that will be in progress at a future moment.","It often shows future background or a longer future process.","In questions, Future Continuous can sound more polite when you ask about plans or arrangements."]}
